title: Rescan a Tote to Reassociate It With Its Designated Location
summary: Use rescanning to properly reassociate a tote with its designated location when a tote removed from the sorter stand was not returned with the same Tote ID to its original position. The source explicitly states rescanning is required in this condition, but the full rescanning workflow is only partially supported by the supplied source evidence.
when_to_use: Use when a tote has been manually removed from the sorter stand and was not returned with the same Tote ID to its original position, causing the tote-to-location association to be disrupted and requiring rescanning to restore the designated location association.
not_for: Do not use as a complete standalone rescanning procedure when the operator does not have access to the documented scanner-based tote handling process, because the full rescan steps are not provided in this source section.
Do not use for unrelated hospital-station barcode exception workflows, AGV recovery workflows, or other HMI fault conditions not tied to sorter tote-position reassociation.

responsibilities: Identify when tote-to-position association has been disrupted at the sorter.
Use the documented scanner-based process available to the user to rescan the tote.
Verify the tote is associated with the intended location after rescanning.
Escalate if reassociation cannot be completed from the documented scanner process.

safety_notes: The supplied source evidence does not identify lockout/tagout requirements for this activity.
Only use the documented scanner-based process available to the user; the full rescanning sequence is not provided in this source section.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the tote being reassociated

access_required: Access to the sorter tote location involved in the mismatch
Access to the Zebra scanner used for sorter operations
system_preconditions: A tote has been manually removed from the sorter stand.
The tote was not returned with the same Tote ID to its original position, or the original tote-position association is otherwise known to be incorrect.
external_references: Sorter scanner operation section of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual
Tote Initialization Procedure section of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual

visual_references.description: Sorter scanner operation image associated with Add Tote, tote barcode scan, tote ID verification, Confirm & Execute, and the note that rescanning is required if the same Tote ID is not returned to the original position.
Tote Initialization Procedure image showing scanner-based pairing of sorter stand position barcode and tote barcode, including successful and unsuccessful scan indications.

steps.title: Identify the tote-position association mismatch
steps.purpose: Confirm that the reassociation condition exists before attempting rescanning.
steps.instruction: Determine whether a tote manually removed from the sorter stand was not returned with the same Tote ID to its original position. Treat this as a tote-to-location association issue requiring rescanning.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms that the tote association has been disrupted and that rescanning is required.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote with the same Tote ID was returned to its original position, or the mismatch condition is clearly identified before proceeding.
steps.failure_condition: The tote was returned to the wrong position, a different Tote ID was returned, or the operator cannot determine whether the original association was preserved.

actions.label: Confirm mismatch condition
actions.exact_action: Identify that the tote was not returned with the same Tote ID to its original position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter scanner operation context showing Add Tote workflow and the note that rescanning is required if the same Tote ID is not returned to its original position.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator cannot determine whether the tote returned is the same Tote ID.
The original sorter position cannot be identified from available information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is manually removed from the sorter stand, it is essential that the tote with the same Tote ID is returned to its original position.
Failure to do so will require rescanning to properly associate the tote with its designated location.

steps.title: Rescan the tote using the documented scanner process
steps.purpose: Restore the tote-to-designated-location association using the scanner-based process referenced by the source.
steps.instruction: Use the documented scanner-based process available to the user to rescan the tote so it can be properly associated with its designated location. Where the available scanner workflow includes sorter destination or tote/position barcode pairing, follow that documented process exactly.
steps.expected_result: The tote is rescanned through the documented process and the system accepts the reassociation attempt.
steps.healthy_condition: The scanner-based process completes successfully and indicates a successful scan or pairing.
steps.failure_condition: The scanner process does not complete, indicates an unsuccessful scan or pairing, or the user lacks the full documented rescanning workflow needed to proceed.

actions.label: Perform tote rescan
actions.exact_action: Use the documented scanner-based process to rescan the tote so it can be properly associated with its designated location.

actions.label: Follow documented barcode pairing workflow if applicable
actions.exact_action: If using the Tote Initialization Procedure workflow, open the application, select "Sorter Destinations," scan the tote sorter stand position barcode, and scan the tote barcode for the tote being placed in the stand.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter scanner operation image tied to Add Tote, tote barcode scan, tote ID verification, and Confirm & Execute.
Tote Initialization Procedure image showing Sorter Destinations selection and scanning of sorter stand position barcode and tote barcode.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented rescanning procedure available to the user is incomplete or unavailable.
The scanner indicates an unsuccessful scan or pairing and repeated rescanning does not resolve it.
The tote cannot be properly associated using the documented scanner process.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Failure to do so will require rescanning to properly associate the tote with its designated location.
Navigate to the Add Tote screen, scan the tote barcode, verify the tote ID is displayed correctly, and press Confirm & Execute so the AGV takes the tote back into the system.

Open the application, select "Sorter Destinations," scan the tote sorter stand position barcode located in the center of the rack on the top rail, and scan the tote barcode for the tote being placed in the stand.
A successful scan pairing causes the scanner to flash green, while an unsuccessful pairing causes a yellow flash and requires rescanning.

steps.title: Verify the tote is associated to the intended location
steps.purpose: Confirm that rescanning restored the intended tote-to-location association.
steps.instruction: Verify through the documented tote identification process that the tote is associated to the intended location after rescanning. If the available workflow includes tote ID display verification or successful scan confirmation, use those indicators.
steps.expected_result: The tote is confirmed to be associated with the intended designated location.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote ID displays correctly or the documented scanner workflow indicates successful association to the intended location.
steps.failure_condition: The tote ID does not verify correctly, the location association remains uncertain, or the reassociation cannot be confirmed from the documented process.

actions.label: Verify tote association
actions.exact_action: Verify through the documented tote identification process that the tote is associated to the intended location after rescanning.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Displayed tote ID verification on the Add Tote workflow before Confirm & Execute.
Successful scan indication for tote/position pairing.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote cannot be confirmed as associated with the intended location.
The tote ID does not display correctly in the documented workflow.
Repeated rescanning does not produce a successful association.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Scan the tote barcode, verify the tote ID is displayed correctly, and press Confirm & Execute so the AGV takes the tote back into the system.
A successful scan pairing causes the scanner to flash green.

success_criteria: The tote is properly reassociated with its designated location.
The documented scanner workflow completes successfully.
The tote identification or scan confirmation indicates the intended association has been restored.
healthy_conditions: The same Tote ID is known and the intended original position is identified.
The scanner process completes with a successful indication.
The tote ID displays correctly during verification.
The tote is confirmed associated to the intended location after rescanning.
failure_conditions: The tote was returned with a different Tote ID or to the wrong position and reassociation is not completed.
The source section does not provide enough detail to execute the full rescanning workflow without another documented scanner process available to the user.
The scanner indicates an unsuccessful scan or pairing.
The tote cannot be confirmed as associated with the intended location after rescanning.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for review if the reassociation cannot be completed using the documented scanner process available to the user.
Escalate if the original tote position or correct Tote ID cannot be determined.
Escalate if repeated rescanning attempts do not produce a successful association.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter scanner operation image for Add Tote, tote barcode scan, tote ID verification, Confirm & Execute, and rescanning context.
Tote Initialization Procedure image for sorter destination selection, position barcode scan, tote barcode scan, and scan success/failure indication.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Failure to do so will require rescanning to properly associate the tote with its designated location.

missing_details: The supplied source section explicitly states rescanning is required but does not provide a complete standalone rescanning procedure for this exact recovery case.
The exact screen name, application state, and confirmation method for reassociation in this specific mismatch scenario are not fully provided in the packet.
No explicit production-stop requirement is provided in the source evidence.
No explicit LOTO requirement is provided in the source evidence.
No time estimate is provided in the source evidence.
